---
title: "Bayesian Inference with MCMC"
subtitle: "Turing.jl Vignette 06"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    code-fold: false
  gfm:
    toc: true
  pdf:
    toc: true
engine: julia
julia:
  project: ".."
---

## Introduction

This vignette demonstrates a complete Bayesian inference pipeline using [Turing.jl](https://turinglang.org/) with an ODE model defined via [ModelingToolkit.jl](https://docs.sciml.ai/ModelingToolkit/stable/). This mirrors the R odin2/dust2/monty workflow: define a model, generate synthetic data, specify priors, run MCMC, and analyse posteriors.

The R companion (in `R/`) uses the same model, data, and priors but with odin2/dust2/monty (stochastic model + particle filter + random walk MCMC).

## Model Definition

```{julia}
using ModelingToolkit
using ModelingToolkit: t_nounits as t, D_nounits as D
using DifferentialEquations
using Turing
using Distributions
using CairoMakie
using Random
using Statistics
using Logging

@parameters β=0.5 γ=0.1 N_pop=1000.0
@variables S(t)=990.0 I(t)=10.0 R(t)=0.0

eqs = [
    D(S) ~ -β * S * I / N_pop,
    D(I) ~ β * S * I / N_pop - γ * I,
    D(R) ~ γ * I,
]

@named sir = ODESystem(eqs, t)
sir = structural_simplify(sir)
```

## Generate Synthetic Data

We simulate the deterministic SIR and add Poisson noise to the daily incidence (ΔR).

```{julia}
prob_true = ODEProblem(sir, [], (0.0, 50.0), [sir.β => 0.5, sir.γ => 0.1])
sol_true = solve(prob_true, Tsit5(); saveat=1.0)

R_vals = sol_true[sir.R]
daily_incidence = max.(diff(R_vals), 1e-6)

Random.seed!(1)
observed = [rand(Poisson(λ)) for λ in daily_incidence]
data_times = collect(sol_true.t[2:end])

println("Observed cases (first 10): ", observed[1:10])
println("Total observed: ", sum(observed))
```

## Set Up Inference

Priors: β ~ Gamma(shape=2, rate=4), γ ~ Gamma(shape=2, rate=20). These are identical to the R monty_dsl priors.

```{julia}
sir_sys = sir

@model function sir_inference(observed, data_times)
    β_est ~ Gamma(2, 1/4)      # shape=2, scale=1/4 (rate=4) → mean=0.5
    γ_est ~ Gamma(2, 1/20)     # shape=2, scale=1/20 (rate=20) → mean=0.1

    prob = ODEProblem(sir_sys, [], (0.0, maximum(data_times)),
                      [sir_sys.β => β_est, sir_sys.γ => γ_est])
    sol = solve(prob, Tsit5(); saveat=data_times)

    if sol.retcode != ReturnCode.Success
        Turing.@addlogprob! -Inf
        return
    end

    R_vals = sol[sir_sys.R]
    R_at_0 = 0.0
    for i in eachindex(observed)
        R_prev = i == 1 ? R_at_0 : R_vals[i - 1]
        λ = max(R_vals[i] - R_prev, 1e-6)
        observed[i] ~ Poisson(λ)
    end
end

model = sir_inference(observed, data_times)
```

## Run MCMC

We use the NUTS sampler (2000 iterations, 1000 warmup).

```{julia}
#| output: false
timing = @timed begin
    chain = with_logger(NullLogger()) do
        sample(model, NUTS(), 2000; progress=false)
    end
end
elapsed = timing.time
println("Sampling complete: 2000 iterations in ", round(elapsed; digits=2), "s")
```

## Results

```{julia}
burnin = 500
β_samples = vec(chain[:β_est])[burnin+1:end]
γ_samples = vec(chain[:γ_est])[burnin+1:end]

println("β: mean = ", round(mean(β_samples); digits=3),
        ", 95% CI = [", round(quantile(β_samples, 0.025); digits=3),
        ", ", round(quantile(β_samples, 0.975); digits=3), "]")
println("γ: mean = ", round(mean(γ_samples); digits=3),
        ", 95% CI = [", round(quantile(γ_samples, 0.025); digits=3),
        ", ", round(quantile(γ_samples, 0.975); digits=3), "]")
println("True: β = 0.5, γ = 0.1")
```

## Posterior Histograms

```{julia}
fig = Figure(size=(700, 350))
ax1 = Axis(fig[1, 1]; xlabel="β", ylabel="Density", title="Posterior: β")
hist!(ax1, β_samples; bins=30, normalization=:pdf, color=(:steelblue, 0.7))
vlines!(ax1, [0.5]; color=:red, linewidth=2, label="True")
axislegend(ax1; position=:rt)

ax2 = Axis(fig[1, 2]; xlabel="γ", ylabel="Density", title="Posterior: γ")
hist!(ax2, γ_samples; bins=30, normalization=:pdf, color=(:steelblue, 0.7))
vlines!(ax2, [0.1]; color=:red, linewidth=2, label="True")
axislegend(ax2; position=:rt)
fig
```

## Trace Plots

```{julia}
fig2 = Figure(size=(700, 400))
ax1 = Axis(fig2[1, 1]; xlabel="Iteration", ylabel="β", title="Trace: β")
lines!(ax1, 1:length(β_samples), β_samples; color=:steelblue, linewidth=0.5)
hlines!(ax1, [0.5]; color=:red, linewidth=2)

ax2 = Axis(fig2[2, 1]; xlabel="Iteration", ylabel="γ", title="Trace: γ")
lines!(ax2, 1:length(γ_samples), γ_samples; color=:steelblue, linewidth=0.5)
hlines!(ax2, [0.1]; color=:red, linewidth=2)
fig2
```

## Effective Sample Size

```{julia}
function ess_simple(x)
    n = length(x)
    max_lag = min(n - 1, 1000)
    μ = mean(x)
    var0 = sum((x .- μ).^2) / n
    var0 == 0 && return Float64(n)
    ρ = zeros(max_lag)
    for k in 1:max_lag
        ρ[k] = sum((x[1:n-k] .- μ) .* (x[k+1:n] .- μ)) / (n * var0)
    end
    tau = 1.0
    for k in 1:2:max_lag-1
        pair = ρ[k] + ρ[k+1]
        pair < 0 && break
        tau += 2 * pair
    end
    return n / tau
end

ess_β = ess_simple(β_samples)
ess_γ = ess_simple(γ_samples)

println("── Performance ──")
println("Sampler:    NUTS (Turing.jl)")
println("Iterations: 2000 (burn-in: ", burnin, ")")
println("Time:       ", round(elapsed; digits=2), " s")
println("ESS (β):    ", round(ess_β; digits=1))
println("ESS (γ):    ", round(ess_γ; digits=1))
println("ESS/s (β):  ", round(ess_β / elapsed; digits=1))
println("ESS/s (γ):  ", round(ess_γ / elapsed; digits=1))
```
